description: A black screen displays text reading "CREATED BY DAVID CRANE & MARTA KAUFFMAN" in a stylized font over a dim, barely visible background. Instrumental music plays.
The screen remains mostly dark before the setting changes to an aerial view of a dense city skyline on a bright day. The music continues.
The view holds on the cityscape, showing numerous tall buildings.
The setting changes to the exterior of a storefront. A large window bears the painted logo "CENTRAL PERK" flanked by two coffee cups. Inside, several people are visible.

Inside the crowded coffee shop, five people sit around a large orange sofa and armchairs, with a coffee table between them. Monica, wearing a beige jacket, sits on the sofa and says, "There's nothing to tell."

Monica gestures slightly as she says, "Just some guy I work with." Joey, seated in an armchair, leans forward and says, "Come on, you're going out with the guy." On-screen text reads "PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS".
Joey continues, "There's got to be something wrong with him." Monica leans back and replies, "All right, Joey, be nice."
Chandler, sitting beside Monica, leans forward slightly.
Chandler gestures with his hand and asks, "So does he have a hump? A hump and a hairpiece."
Monica looks toward Chandler, while Phoebe sits beside her listening.
Phoebe raises a hand and asks, "Wait, does he eat chalk?" Monica looks toward her.
Phoebe continues, gesturing with both hands, "Just because I don't want her to go through what I went through with Carl."
Monica leans forward and says, "Okay, everybody relax. Relax," gesturing with her hands. On-screen text reads "WRITTEN BY MARTA KAUFFMAN & DAVID CRANE".
Monica continues, "This is not even a date," shaking her head slightly.
Monica adds, "It's not? It's just two people going out to dinner and not having sex," moving her hands for emphasis. On-screen text reads "DIRECTED BY JAMES BURROWS".
Chandler looks forward, his expression steady.
Chandler says, "Sounds like a date to me," looking downward. Audience laughter is audible.
Upbeat music plays briefly. The group remains seated around the table as Chandler speaks.
Chandler leans back on the sofa and says, "All right, so I'm back in high school," making a small hand gesture.
Chandler continues, "I'm standing in the middle of the cafeteria and I realize I am totally naked." Audience laughter follows.
Monica and Phoebe look toward Chandler, reacting to his statement.
Chandler, resting a hand near his chin, says, "Then I look down and I realize there is a phone," moving his hand downward.
Chandler points a finger downward and maintains his posture.
Chandler looks upward and says, "there." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey, sitting in the armchair with a blue mug, looks closely at Chandler.
Joey gestures and says, "Instead of..." Chandler points at him and finishes, "that's right?"
The group continues listening. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler, resting an arm on the sofa back, says, "All of a sudden, the phone starts to ring," gesturing with his hands.
Chandler shrugs slightly, holding his arms open.
Chandler continues, "Now I don't know what to do. Everybody starts looking at me," keeping his hands spread.
Monica looks at Chandler and asks, "They weren't looking at you before?"
Chandler looks at Monica with a steady gaze. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler gestures with his hands and says, "Finally, I figure I'd better answer it."
Chandler pauses, his hands raised.
Chandler drops his hands slightly and says, "And it turns out it's my mother."
Joey looks at Chandler.
Chandler leans his head back slightly and says, "Which is very, very weird because she never calls." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross enters the coffee shop carrying a closed umbrella, his head down and posture slumped.
Ross comes to a stop near the sofa, continuing to look downward.
Ross says, "Hi," his pitch lower than the preceding dialogue.
Ross remains standing still, holding his umbrella. Joey turns toward the others.
Joey, gesturing with his hand, says, "This guy says, Hello, I want to kill myself." Audience laughter is heard.
Monica reaches a hand out toward Ross.
Monica asks, "You okay, sweetie?" Ross gestures to his chest and says, "I just feel like someone reached down my throat..."
Ross continues, "...grabbed my small intestine, pulled it out of my mouth and..." making pulling motions with his hand near his mouth.
Ross finishes, "tied it around my neck," making a tying motion around his neck.
Chandler holds out a cookie toward Ross and asks, "Cookie?"
Ross looks at Chandler, who keeps the cookie extended. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica says, "Carol moved her stuff out today."
She stands up from the sofa, says, "Let me get you some," and walks away from the seating area.
Ross moves around the table and sits on the sofa next to Phoebe, holding his umbrella across his lap.
Phoebe leans toward Ross, moving her hands quickly in circular motions around his head and shoulders.
Ross leans away from Phoebe, raises a hand to block her, and says, "No, no, don't. Stop cleansing my aura."
Ross waves his hand dismissively.
Ross keeps his hands raised and says, "Don't. Just leave my aura alone, okay?"
Phoebe pulls her hands back, sits upright, and replies, "Fine. Be murky." Audience laughter is heard.
Ross, looking at the others, says, "I'll be fine, all right?"
Ross says, "Really, everyone. I hope she'll be very happy." An off-screen female voice replies, "No, you don't."
Ross shakes his head and says, "No, I don't. To hell with her. She left me."
Joey sits in the armchair, holding his mug with both hands.
Joey looks at Ross and asks, "And you never knew she was a lesbian?"
Ross stares at Joey with a steady gaze. Phoebe sits quietly next to Ross. Audience laughter is heard.
Ross replies, "No," maintaining his gaze on Joey.
Ross adds, "Okay?" raising his hands slightly.
Ross lowers his hands and turns his head slightly.
Ross gestures outward with both hands and asks, "Why does everyone keep fixating on that?", his volume rising on "fixating".
Joey lowers his gaze briefly.
Ross continues, "She didn't know. How should I know?" gesturing toward himself.
Chandler sits on the sofa, leaning back and looking straight ahead.
Chandler says, "Sometimes I wish I was a lesbian," keeping his gaze forward. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross, Phoebe, and Joey turn their heads to look at Chandler. Laughter continues.
Chandler looks around at the group and asks, "Did I say that out loud?"
Chandler maintains his gaze as audience laughter continues.
In a coffee shop, Ross sits on a sofa, holding a yellow mug and looking down. Monica walks toward him.
Ross slightly raises his head and says, "I told mom and dad last night. They they seemed to take it pretty well."
Monica stops behind the sofa, gesturing with her hands, and asks, "Oh, really? So that hysterical phone call I got from a woman"
Monica sits on the armrest of the sofa, leans toward Ross, and says, "sobbing at 03:00 a.m. I'll never have grandchildren, I'll never have grandchildren was what?"
Ross looks at Monica and asks, "A wrong number?"
Ross looks away and lowers his head.
Ross says, "Sorry."
Joey, sitting in an armchair nearby, holds a blue mug and says, "All right, ross, look. You're feeling a lot of pain right now."
Joey gestures with his hands, saying, "You're angry. You're hurting."
Joey continues, "Can I tell you what the answer is? Strip joint."
Ross turns his head away from Joey.
Joey gestures toward Ross and says, "Come on, you're single. Have some hormones."
Ross raises a hand and says, "But I don't want to be single, okay?"
Ross lowers his hand and says, "I just want to be married again."
Rachel, wearing a wet wedding dress, walks quickly into the coffee shop. The group turns to look at her.
Chandler extends his arm outward and says, "And I just want a million dollars," then brings both hands to his chest.
Rachel walks toward the seating area as Monica stands up from the armrest.
Monica walks over to Rachel.
Monica asks, "Rachel?" Rachel hugs Monica and says, "Oh, God, Monica. Hi. Thank God."
Rachel pulls back from the hug and says, "I just went to your building and you weren't there. And then this guy with a big hammer said that"
Rachel touches Monica's arm and says, "you might be here. And you are. You are."
Monica asks, "Can I get you some coffee?"
Rachel replies, "Decaf."
Monica turns toward the group seated on the sofa.
Monica says, "Hey, everybody."
Monica gestures to Rachel and says, "This is Rachel. And another Lincoln high survivor. This is everybody."
Monica points to each person in turn and says, "This is Chandler and Phoebe and Joey. And you remember"
Monica motions toward Ross and says, "my brother, Ross."
Rachel says, "Sure."
Ross stands up holding a black umbrella, which suddenly springs open. Joey leans back in his armchair in response.
Ross closes the umbrella and sits down. Rachel sits on the sofa next to him.
Monica, seated on the armrest again, asks, "So you want to tell us now, or are we waiting for four wet bridesmaids?"
Rachel looks down and takes a breath.
Rachel says, "Oh, God. Well, it started about a half hour before the wedding."
Rachel gestures with her hands, saying, "I was in this room where we were keeping all the presents."
Rachel says, "And I was looking at this gravy boat this really gorgeous, lamoge gravy boat."
Rachel continues, "When all of a sudden I"
A man places a mug into Rachel's hands. She says, "realized sweet. And realized"
Rachel holds the mug, looking forward.
Rachel says, "I realized that I was more turned on by this gravy boat than by Barry."
Rachel continues, "And then I got really freaked out. And that's"
Rachel says, "when it hit me how much Barry looks like."
Rachel taps her head with her fingers.
Rachel says, "I always know he looked familiar. But"
Rachel holds the mug and moves her hands slightly as she looks toward the others.
Rachel says, "anyway, I just had to get out of there and I started wondering"
Rachel continues, "why am I doing this? And who am I doing this for? So"
Rachel says, "anyway, I just didn't know where to go. And I know that you and I have kind of drifted apart"
Rachel says, "but you're the only person I knew"
Rachel says, "who lived here in the city who wasn't invited to the wedding."
Monica says, "Oh, I was kind of hoping that wouldn't be an issue."
The scene changes to the exterior of a brick building. The lower portion of the building and the street level are then shown.
The setting changes to a living room. The group is seated around a coffee table, watching a television that displays a man and a woman.
On the television, a woman and a man are shown as a voice sings, "Criminalisala De Marcela Singing".
Monica points to the television and says, "all right, I'm guessing that he bought her"
Monica lowers her hand and says, "the big pipe organ and she's really not happy about it."
On the television, the woman gestures with her hands.
Chandler gestures toward the television and says, "Tuna. Egg salad, desa."
Ross leans forward slightly.
Ross looks toward Chandler and says, "I'll have whatever Christine is having."
Rachel is seen in the kitchen, pacing while holding a phone to her ear.
Rachel says, "Daddy, I just I can't marry him."
Rachel closes her eyes.
Rachel says, "I'm sorry."
Rachel touches her head with her free hand.
Rachel says, "I just don't love him."
Rachel lowers her hand and looks down.
Rachel says, "Well, it matters to me."
A television screen shows two women on a staircase, with Spanish dialogue playing.

In an apartment living room, Monica, Phoebe, Chandler, and Joey sit on and around a sofa facing the television. Ross stands behind the sofa, and Rachel, wearing a wedding dress, stands in the kitchen area. "If I let go of my hair, my hip will fall off," Chandler says. Audience laughter is audible.
The television screen shows the two women again.
"She should not be wearing those pants," Chandler says. On the television, one woman grabs the other by the shoulders.
Joey gestures toward the television.
"I'd say push her down the stairs. Push her down," Joey says, pointing at the television. He leans forward and chants as the others join in.
The group continues chanting.
"Push," they chant. The television shows one woman pushing the other down the stairs, and the group claps. Audience laughter is audible.
The group continues clapping. Rachel paces in the kitchen, holding a cordless phone to her ear.
"Come on, Daddy, listen to me," Rachel says, gesturing with her free hand. Ross opens the refrigerator behind her.
"It's like it's like all of my life everyone has always told me, you're a," Rachel continues, pacing slightly. Ross stands by the refrigerator.
"Shoe. You're a shoe," Rachel says, her volume rising slightly. Ross stands by the open refrigerator holding a glass.
"You're a shoe. You're a shoe," Rachel continues, gesturing outward with her hand.
"And then today I just stopped and I said what if I don't want to be a shoe?" Rachel says, gesturing outward.
"What if I want to be a purse?" Rachel asks, looking away from the phone briefly before continuing, her hand raised.
Rachel continues speaking on the phone.
"Or a hat?" Rachel asks, leaning forward slightly.
Rachel looks downward, her hand still raised, while Ross stands nearby.

"No, I don't want you to buy me a hat. I'm saying that I am a hat. It's a metaphor, Daddy," Rachel says, her pitch rising on the final phrase. She lowers her hand and shakes her head. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks at Rachel, his eyebrows slightly raised.
"You can see where you'd have trouble," Ross says, holding his glass. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel paces away from Ross, turning her back to him. Ross walks toward the living area.
"Look, Daddy, it's my life," Rachel says, touching her chest with her free hand.
Rachel lowers her hand and looks down.
"Well, maybe I'll just stay here with Monica," Rachel says, gesturing outward.
Monica, seated in a chair in the living area, looks toward Rachel.
"Well, I guess we've established she's staying here with Monica," Monica says, gesturing with both hands. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel paces back toward the kitchen counter, touching her chest.
"Well, maybe that's my decision," Rachel says, her gaze directed downward.
Rachel lowers her hand and stands still.
"Well, maybe I don't need your money," Rachel says, her pitch rising slightly.
"Wait. I said maybe," Rachel says quickly, turning her head and raising her hand. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers the phone and presses buttons on it. A series of cityscape shots show a bridge and tall buildings.
Inside the apartment, Joey stands holding a sandwich. Rachel sits on the sofa, breathing heavily into a paper bag, while Monica rubs her back. Phoebe leans over the back of the sofa.
"It's breathe," Monica says, continuing to rub Rachel's back.
"Breathe. That's it. Just try to think of nice, calm things," Monica says, looking at Rachel.
Phoebe leans closer to Rachel.
"Raindrops on roses and," Phoebe sings, her head swaying slightly.
Rachel continues breathing into the bag. Monica looks at Phoebe.
"Doorbells and sleigh bells and sing with mittens," Phoebe sings, gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe continues moving her hands as Rachel breathes into the bag.
"Something and noodles with strings," Phoebe sings. Rachel pulls the bag away from her face and says, "I'm all better now." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel rubs her leg, and Phoebe pats her shoulder.
"I have," Phoebe says, standing up straight.
Joey walks behind the sofa toward the kitchen. Rachel touches her forehead.
"Okay, look, this is probably for the best. You know, independence," Monica says, leaning closer to Rachel.
"taking control of your life. The whole hat thing," Monica continues, her hands moving slightly as she speaks.
Joey stands behind the sofa and says, "And hey, you need anything, you can always come to Joey," gesturing to himself.
Joey gestures with his sandwich.
"Me and Chandler live right across the hall, and he's away a lot," Joey says, pointing his sandwich toward Rachel. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks at Joey with her eyebrows drawn together.
"Joey, stop hitting on her. It's her wedding day," Monica says. Joey gestures outward and asks, "What, like there's a rule or something?" Audience laughter is audible.
A buzzer sounds loudly. Chandler, standing in the kitchen area, jumps slightly and turns toward the door.
"I got it," Chandler says, walking toward the door.
Chandler reaches the intercom on the wall.
Chandler presses a button on the intercom and says, "Please don't do that again. It's a horrible sound," leaning against the wall. Audience laughter is audible.
A voice from the intercom speaks indistinctly.
"It's Paul," the intercom voice says. "Oh, God," Monica says, her eyes widening.
"Is it 630?" Monica asks, quickly checking her watch.
Monica stands up and moves toward the kitchen.
"Buz him in," Monica says. Ross asks, "Who's Paul? Paul, the wine guy, Paul?" gesturing with his hand.
"Maybe," Monica says. "Wait a minute. You're not a real date tonight is with Paul, the wine guy?" Joey asks, stepping forward.
"He finally asked you out?" Ross asks, pointing at Monica.
"Yes," Monica says. "Oh, this is a dear diary," Ross says, raising his hands.
Ross steps closer to Monica, placing his hands on her waist.
"Wait, wait," Rachel says, sitting on the sofa.
Rachel turns to look at Monica.
"I can cancel," Rachel says. "Oh, God. Please, no, go. I'll be fine," Monica says, waving her hands.
Monica turns to Ross, who is standing beside her.
"Ross, are you okay? I mean, do you want me to stay?" Monica asks, touching his arm.
Ross looks down slightly, holding his glass.
"That'd be good," Ross says softly. Audience laughter is audible.
Monica looks at him, then steps back slightly.
"Really?" Monica asks. "No, go on. It's Paul. The wine guy?" Ross replies, gesturing outward. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross walks toward the door, passing Phoebe in the kitchen area.
"Does that mean does he sell it, drink it, or he just complains a lot?" Chandler asks, holding a coffee mug.
Chandler stands in the kitchen looking forward.

In an apartment living room, Joey, Ross, Chandler, Phoebe, and Rachel, who wears an off-the-shoulder white wedding dress, stand looking toward the open front door. Monica stands by the door, and Paul steps inside.
"Hi. Come in. Paul, this is everybody," Monica says, gesturing toward the group.
The group murmurs greetings, with Joey and Chandler audible saying, "Paul the wine guy." Audience laughter is audible as Paul smiles and places his hands in his pockets.
Joey leans forward, putting a hand on his chest, and says, "I'm sorry, I didn't catch your name. Paul, was it?" Audience laughter sounds.
Joey smiles and Chandler laughs. Monica steps in front of Paul and touches his arm.
"Okay, I'll be right back. I've just got to go a wandering," Monica says, touching Paul's chest before pausing.
Monica lowers her hand.
"Change. Okay. Sit down 2 seconds," Monica says, holding up two fingers and guiding Paul toward the living area before walking away.
Paul steps further into the room as Joey points at him. In the kitchen area, Phoebe pulls at her eyelashes while Rachel stands nearby holding a mug.
Phoebe looks at her fingers and says, "Oh, I just pulled out four eyelashes. That can't be good." Rachel watches her. Audience laughter plays.
Joey walks over to Paul, who is now sitting on the arm of a sofa. Joey approaches him with a slight smile.
"Hey, Paul," Joey says, looking down at Paul.
Paul turns his head to look up at Joey.
"Here's a little tip," Joey continues, leaning slightly toward Paul.
"She really likes it when you rub her neck in the same spot over and over and over again," Joey says, gesturing with his hand as Paul nods.
"...till it starts to get a little red," Joey finishes, pointing a finger at Paul. From off-screen, Monica shouts, "Shut up, Joey!" and audience laughter plays.
Joey turns and walks away from Paul. Ross approaches Rachel in the kitchen, holding a glass.
"So, Rachel, what are you up to tonight?" Ross asks, standing near her. Rachel looks up at him.
"Well, I was kind of supposed to be headed for Aruba on my honeymoon. So," Rachel replies, looking upward.
"nothing," Rachel adds, lowering her gaze.
Ross touches his forehead and looks down as audience laughter sounds.
"Right?" Ross says, nodding slightly.
"You're not even getting your no," Ross continues, looking at Rachel.
"No Avoke Aruba this time of year," Ross says, gesturing slightly with his glass.
Ross pauses and looks downward.
"Talk about your," Ross says, raising his glass.
Rachel stares at Ross with her lips parted.
"big lizards," Ross adds, making a small gesture with his other hand.
Ross looks down and gestures with his free hand. Rachel continues staring at him. Audience laughter plays.
"Anyway, if you don't feel like being alone tonight," Ross says, gesturing toward Joey and Chandler.
"...Joey and Chandler are coming over to help me put together my new furniture," Ross continues. "Yes," Chandler adds, stepping forward.
"and we're very excited about it," Chandler says, standing next to Joey with his hands on his hips.
Rachel looks at Joey and Chandler.
"Actually, thanks, but I think I'm just going to hang out here tonight. It's been a long," Rachel says, looking at Ross.
"oh, sure. Okay, sure," Ross replies, turning away slightly. Rachel stands up.
"Hey, Phoebe, do you want to help?" Ross asks, looking toward the kitchen window. "Oh, I wish I could, but I don't want to," Phoebe replies.
Joey and Chandler look at Phoebe with slight frowns as audience laughter sounds. The setting changes to a subway train passing on elevated tracks.
The setting changes to a subway station staircase, where Phoebe stands playing an acoustic guitar. People walk past her on the stairs.
"Love is sweet as summer showers," Phoebe sings, strumming her guitar as more people walk past her.
"...love is wondrous work of art but your," Phoebe continues singing, looking toward the passersby.
"love oh, your love your love," Phoebe sings, maintaining her strumming rhythm.
A man drops a coin into Phoebe's open guitar case.
"is like a giant pigeon," Phoebe sings, her pitch rising.
Phoebe continues playing her guitar, looking downward briefly.
"prepping on my heart," Phoebe sings.
A man drops money into the case as he walks by.
"thank you, la la," Phoebe sings, glancing down at the case.
Audience laughter sounds. The setting changes to an exterior nighttime view of city skyscrapers with lit windows.
The view shifts to a tall apartment building exterior at night. The setting then changes to an apartment interior where Ross, Joey, and Chandler are assembling furniture amid cardboard boxes.
Ross squats on the floor holding a paper instruction sheet. "I'm supposed to attach a brackety thing to the side things," he says, gesturing with his hand.
"...using a bunch of these little worm guys," Ross adds, pointing at the paper. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks down at the paper.
"I have no brackety thing. I see no worm guys whatsoever," Ross says, lowering the paper and extending his hand outward.
"...and I cannot feel my legs," Ross finishes, his voice strained. He rubs his legs as audience laughter sounds.
Joey and Chandler walk over to a partially assembled shelving unit. Ross stands up and stretches his legs.
"I'm thinking. We got a bookcase here. It's a beautiful thing," Joey says, placing his hand on the metal frame.
Joey steps back and Chandler picks up a small black metal piece from the floor.
"What's this?" Joey asks. Chandler holds up the piece and says, "I would have to say that it's an L shaped bracket."
Joey and Chandler look at each other. Audience laughter plays.
"Which goes where?" Joey asks.
"I have no idea," Chandler replies, shaking his head.
Joey tosses a piece into a wicker basket on the floor. Chandler watches him.
"Done with the bookcase," Joey says, wiping his hands together.
Chandler places his hands on his hips. Ross stands in the background looking at a piece of paper. Audience laughter continues until the clip ends.
In an apartment living room with partially unpacked boxes, Joey and Chandler stand facing Ross. Ross holds a beer can and says, "This was Carol's favorite beer."
Ross looks down at the can while Joey and Chandler watch him.
Ross gestures with the hand holding the can. "She always drank it out of the can. I should have," he says, with audience laughter overlapping his words.
Ross holds the can and looks away. Joey points at him.
Joey says, "Hey, if you're going to start with that stuff, we're out of here."
Chandler gestures toward the boxes on the floor, saying, "Yes, please don't spoil all this fun." Audience laughter occurs.
Joey gestures with one hand. "Ross, let me ask you a question," he says.
Joey counts on his fingers as he says, "She got the furniture, the stereo, the good TV what did you get?"
Ross looks at them and says, "You guys?" Audience laughter follows. Joey tilts his head back, saying, "Oh, God." Chandler leans forward and says, "You got."
The setting changes to a restaurant. Monica and Paul sit across from each other at a small table. Paul gestures with his hands and says, "Oh, my God. Oh, my God. I know. I'm such an,"
Paul lowers his head slightly and says, "idiot."
Monica gestures with a spoon as she says, "I guess I should have caught on when you started going to the dentist four and five times a week. I mean, how clean can teeth"
Monica continues, "get? My brother's going through that right now. He's such a mess."
Monica leans slightly forward and asks, "How did you get through it?" Paul responds, "You might try accidentally breaking"
Paul rests his hands on the table. "something valuable of hers," he says.
Monica smiles and asks, "Say her leg."
Paul laughs and points at Monica, then rests his hands on the table. Audience laughter occurs.
Paul leans forward, "Me, I went for the watch." Monica replies, "You actually broke her watch?" Paul nods. "Wow," Monica adds.
Monica points to herself and says, "The worst thing that I ever did was I shredded my old boyfriend's favorite bath towel."
Paul leans back slightly. "OOH. Steer clear of you," he says. Audience laughter is audible.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Rachel stands holding a cordless phone.
Rachel paces slightly with the phone to her ear. "Barry, I'm sorry. I'm so sorry," she says.

Rachel places a hand on her chest. "I know you probably think this is all about what I said the other night about you making love with your socks on," she says, gesturing with her free hand. Audience laughter occurs.
Rachel continues, "but it isn't. It isn't. It's about me. And I didn't," then lowers the phone.
Rachel stares at the phone, then pulls out its antenna.
Rachel puts the phone back to her ear and places her hand on her hip, looking around the room.
Rachel says into the phone, "Hi. Machine cut me off again. Anyway,"
Rachel walks over to a coffee table, stepping around furniture while holding the phone. Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel brushes her hair back with her free hand. "look," she says.
Rachel lowers her hand and holds the phone to her ear.
Rachel continues, "I know that some girl is going to be incredibly lucky to become Mrs."
Rachel says, "Barry Finkel, but it isn't me."
Rachel lowers her gaze and says, "It's not me."

Rachel gestures with her hand. "And not that I have any idea who me is right now, but you just have to give me a chance to," she says, then pulls the phone away from her ear. Audience laughter follows.
Rachel stares at the phone. The setting changes back to the apartment with Ross, Chandler, and Joey.
Ross holds his arms out wide. "I'm divorced," he says loudly.
Ross continues, "I'm only 26 and I'm divorced." Audience laughter occurs as he speaks.
Joey, sitting on the floor, looks up at Ross and says, "Shut up."
Chandler, sitting on the floor holding a piece of paper, looks up and says, "Must stop."
Joey throws down some packaging material while Chandler tears the piece of paper in half. Ross stands in the background.
Ross gestures toward the mess on the floor. An uncertain speaker says, "Shut up."
Chandler gestures with his hands while sitting on the floor. "Got to understand, between us, we haven't had a relationship that," he says.
Chandler continues, "lasted longer than a mento," with audience laughter following.
Ross looks at Chandler. Joey and Chandler stand up.
Chandler stands and points at Ross. "You, however, have had the love of a woman for four years," he says.
Chandler steps toward Ross, gesturing with his hands. "Four years of closeness and sharing, at," he continues.
Chandler sweeps his arm to the side. "the end of which she ripped your heart out. And that is why we don't do it," he says. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks down at his beer can.
Ross looks at Chandler and says, "I don't think that was my point." Audience laughter follows.
The screen cuts to black.
The screen remains black until the clip ends.
